name: Aider (AI Pair Programming in Terminal)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Paul Gauthier; LLM provider costs apply

auth.notes: OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini / local model API keys

agent_readiness.notes: Terminal-based AI coding assistant. aider --model claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219. Adds files to context: /add src/app.py. Automatic git commits after each change. Repo map for codebase understanding. Multi-file coordinated edits. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, local via Ollama. Architect mode (planning) + Editor mode (implementing). /voice for speech input. Linter integration. Highly ranked on SWE-bench. Most popular open-source AI coding tool.

gotchas: Costs can add up with many file edits (large context windows)
Best with Claude Sonnet/Opus or GPT-4o (weaker models produce worse edits)
Automatic commits can clutter git history
Context window limits affect large codebases
best_when: Developers wanting AI pair programmer in terminal
Projects with git where auto-commits are acceptable
Teams using Claude/GPT-4 wanting open-source alternative to Cursor
summary: Aider is the most popular open-source AI pair programming tool for the terminal. aider --model claude-3-7-sonnet. Add files to context, chat to implement features, automatic git commits. Repo map for codebase understanding. Multi-file coordinated edits. Supports Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama. Architect + Editor dual-mode. Highly ranked on SWE-bench. Best open-source alternative to Cursor for terminal-first developers.
